clusters.title: Support Macro Development and Structure
clusters.summary: A collection of notes on creating, organizing, and structuring technical support macros for efficient customer issue diagnosis and response.
keyPoints: Macros should be calm, technical, and specific about the next diagnostic step.
Group macros by failure mode, not by incoming channel.
Need reusable replies that route users to the right diagnostic step quickly.
Reply structure should include symptom, likely cause, next check, and escalation rule.
Examples include macros for permission reset, knowledge root missing, and AI not configured.
Verification of retention windows and filter rules is part of the process.
clusters.reason: All six captures are tightly related to the creation, structure, and organization of technical support macros, forming a coherent theme not directly covered by existing topics.
